topic: Overview evaluate-preface — mechanical fixes verification (EXP-264 rework of EXP-257)
our_argument_summary: EXP-264 reapplies EXP-257 substance (5-question methodology preface promoting eg-evaluate to scannable Overview block with one-click drill-downs to verdict buckets) with three mechanical fixes baked in: (a) EP2 CSS anchor re-anchored from `.verdict-bars .vb-caption` to `.ds-verdict-bars .ds-vb-caption` to match the post-ISS-1573 ds- prefix migration; (b) all 9 new CSS classes ds- prefixed in both segments; (c) three text-color declarations switched from var(--ink-3) to var(--ink-2) to clear dark-mode WCAG AA. Two minor c1 fixes also folded in: ASCII apostrophe in Q2, caption rephrased to 'Read the long form ->'. Decider integration verified — section appears once in build-scripts/generate-html.js and once in built docs/index.html; zero unprefixed selector leaks.

dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: Pure static HTML/CSS additive insert into the Overview tab. No template variables, no JS computation, no data dependencies. The 5 link targets (#stdmodel, #selfcon, #refuted, #misleading, #dedup-analysis) are anchor links to verdict-bucket sections in the wins tab; #eg-evaluate is the long-form preface inside the evaluate tab. Each link uses showTab() to switch tabs before navigating to the anchor.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: Static — all 5 question texts and link targets baked into the template. Substance traceable to EXP-257 c1 review (queue_id 268).
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: Only one numerical discrepancy found, in the EXP-264 wcag_audit_after_fix block — see holes_found[0]. Not a code defect; documentation accuracy issue.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: EXP-264 is a structural rework of HTML/CSS in our review. monitor.py is dome-side; this is review-side methodology. No monitor.py touch.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Not a dome-WIN; this is review-side content.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: Not a dome-WIN; this is review-side content.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: Not a dome-WIN; this is review-side content.

pre_apply_assertions_verified.EP1_anchor_unique: grep -c on '<section role="region" class="accuracy-math-section" aria-labelledby="accuracy-math-heading">' returns 1 in build-scripts/generate-html.js — verified
pre_apply_assertions_verified.idempotency_was_satisfied: Pre-apply state had grep -c on '\.ds-evaluate-preface\b' = 0 (cannot directly verify retroactively post-apply, but post-apply count is consistent with single application: 1 HTML <section>, 14 distinct CSS rules in template)

post_apply_assertions_verified.ds_evaluate_preface_class_present: 1 occurrence in build-scripts/generate-html.js and 1 occurrence in docs/index.html — verified
post_apply_assertions_verified.no_unprefixed_leak: 0 in build-scripts/generate-html.js and 0 in docs/index.html for the regex pattern matching unprefixed evaluate-preface, ep-heading, ep-caption, ep-jump, ep-rows, ep-row, ep-num, ep-q, ep-where — verified clean
post_apply_assertions_verified.five_link_targets_resolve: All five (#stdmodel, #selfcon, #refuted, #misleading, #dedup-analysis) and the caption target (#eg-evaluate) appear as id= attributes in docs/index.html (1 each) — verified
post_apply_assertions_verified.q2_ascii_apostrophe: 'Can the prediction be derived from the model\u0027s own parameters?' — ASCII U+0027 confirmed in source and built HTML; matches existing eg-evaluate body copy
post_apply_assertions_verified.caption_text: 'Read the long form →' confirmed in source and built; 'Full preface' count = 0 (no leak from EXP-257 draft)

ink2_dark_on_cardbg_dark.method: sRGB to linear (gamma 2.4 with 0.03928 break, weights 0.2126/0.7152/0.0722) then contrast (L1+0.05)/(L2+0.05); colors hex #b8b5ad on #222240

ink3_dark_on_cardbg_dark.method: same as above; colors hex #8a8680 on #222240
ink3_dark_on_cardbg_dark.verdict: MATCH — confirms ink-3 dark-mode FAIL that motivated the swap

ink2_light_on_cardbg_light.verdict: MISMATCH (analyst calc off by ~52%) — but conclusion 'passes AA with margin' is unchanged and is in fact even stronger than claimed. The audit-document numerical error does not affect patch correctness; flagged in holes_found[0] as a minor documentation accuracy issue.

holes_found.description: EXP-264 wcag_audit_after_fix.light_mode.ink_2_on_card_bg states 7.69:1; independent re-derivation (sRGB to linear to relative luminance, contrast formula (L1+0.05)/(L2+0.05)) gives 11.65:1 for #3A352D on #fafafa. The other three values in the audit block (dark ink-2: 7.48, dark ink-3: 4.23, light ink-3: 5.36) all match independent calculation exactly. Conclusion (passes AA, switching from ink-3 to ink-2 raises margin) is unchanged and in fact stronger than stated. This is purely a numerical accuracy issue in the audit-trail JSON, not a defect in the integrated patch.
holes_found.recommendation: Update wcag_audit_after_fix.light_mode.ink_2_on_card_bg in monitor/analyst/expansions/EXP-264-overview-evaluate-preface-mechanical.json from '7.69:1' to '11.65:1'. Optional, audit-only — patch itself is correct. Severity is minor because the value is recorded in a closed expansion file that no live page reads from.

holes_found.description: Caption rephrasing 'Read the long form ->' honestly removes the 'Full preface ->' over-promise that implied one-click access, but does not telegraph that arriving on the evaluate tab requires a further click on the <summary> element to expand the collapsed <details id="eg-evaluate"> block. A first-time reader may land on the evaluate tab, see the summary collapsed (showing only the TLDR sentence), and assume the page has not loaded the long-form prose. The wording 'long form' does imply depth — but does not imply 'click summary to expand'. Mitigated somewhat by the eg-evaluate ps-tldr explicitly recapitulating the same 5 questions in prose, so a reader who only sees the summary still gets a coherent answer to the same questions.
holes_found.recommendation: Two reasonable resolutions, neither blocking: (a) accept the de-facto position — the TLDR shown after click is itself a coherent answer to the 5 questions; (b) modify showTab() to auto-expand the destination <details> when the URL hash arrives at #eg-evaluate. Option (b) expands scope beyond EXP-264 and is better tracked as a separate ISS- candidate if reader-test feedback surfaces a problem. No action required for EXP-264 close-out.

stronger_arguments.description: Above-fold acceptance criterion is fragile under the EXP-271 (queue_id 280) integration that landed within hours of this review. EXP-264 acceptance criterion #9 reads 'Above-the-fold check on 1440x900: thesis-hero (EXP-255) remains visible without scrolling; ds-evaluate-preface starts visible or just at the fold.' Built HTML order is now: ds-verdict-bars (line 347) -> falsifiability-module (397) -> ds-thesis-hero (425) -> ds-evaluate-preface (457) -> accuracy-math-section (488). EXP-271 (per queue_id 280 reason text) reorders Overview to thesis-hero -> bar chart -> module, which would place ds-evaluate-preface even further down the visual flow. Whether the acceptance criterion still holds is empirical and should be re-verified against the post-EXP-271 state. EXP-271 is itself in the queue (queue_id 280) and not yet curmudgeon-reviewed, so the post-integration state may still shift.
sources: queue_id 280 (EXP-271 integrated 2026-04-28T01:26Z) — visual order fix supersedes EXP-264 placement assumption

stronger_arguments.description: The 5 questions framing claims portability ('From this review or any other'), but the routing is tightly coupled to dome-model failure modes. A defender could argue the question selection itself is loaded — Q3 'Is the cited data accurately represented?' routes to Refuted by Data, Q5 'Are the same data being counted multiple times?' routes to De-Duplication Analysis. Neither question is dome-specific in phrasing, but their selection (vs. e.g. 'Are the predictions genuinely prospective?' or 'Is the model falsifiable in principle vs. in practice?') reflects what we found in this review. This is a pragmatic editorial choice — the questions themselves are universal, but the bucket-selection mirrors our findings. Worth acknowledging in any future expansion of the framework rather than claiming pure neutrality.

kernel_of_truth.description: EXP-264 is a clean mechanical rework that honors three project invariants the parent draft predated or missed: (1) ISS-1573 ds- prefix invariant on new design-system classes; (2) ISS-1580 dark-mode WCAG AA on text colors; (3) anchor-staleness loud-fail via pre-apply grep assertions. The substance survives the rework; the defects flagged in c1 (queue_id 268) are all addressed; both built-HTML and source-template are leak-free.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: N/A — this is review-side content, not a dome WIN. The kernel here is 'the patch is mechanically correct' and that conclusion is upheld.

deeper_analysis: This review is an explicit c2 verify-focus pass over a c1-approved substance package (EXP-257) that was reworked into EXP-264 strictly to fix three mechanical defects. The decider correctly classified the rework as 'expansion-rework' and routed it back to curmudgeon for verification. All seven items in EXP-264 curmudgeon_review_focus_for_c2 were checked and pass: (1) EP2 anchor unique in current main, (2) all 9 ds- prefix classes land cleanly, (3) three ink-2 swaps present and contrast claims independently reproduce on three of four values, (4) Q2 ASCII apostrophe confirmed, (5) caption text correct, (6) decider_instructions captures the c1-stronger-argument-2 sequencing recommendation, (7) border-left:3px var(--ink-3) intentionally retained for visual differentiation from EXP-256 falsifiability-module (--accent left rail). The one numerical mismatch (light-mode ink-2 value of 7.69 vs computed 11.65) does not affect the verdict because both pass AA with margin; the conclusion holds even more strongly than claimed. No verdict change. No fresh defense_survives count needed — EXP-264 substance is unchanged from EXP-257 c1 where defense_survives was 0.

citations_verified: ISS-1573 (ds- prefix invariant) — closed in closed-issues.json
ISS-1580 (dark-mode WCAG AA on EXP-256) — referenced as open precedent
EP1 anchor '<section role="region" class="accuracy-math-section" aria-labelledby="accuracy-math-heading">' (1 occurrence in build-scripts/generate-html.js)
All 5 related issues (ISS-1559, 1585, 1586, 1587, 1588) confirmed in closed-issues.json
citations_unchecked: queue_id 280 EXP-271 — referenced as a placement-assumption-invalidator but not yet curmudgeon-reviewed (separate queue item)

verify_focus_status.notes: All seven items in curmudgeon_review_focus_for_c2 verified. Substance routing identical to c1-approved package. Three minor surface-level observations recorded (audit numerical mismatch, caption click-to-expand gap, post-EXP-271 above-fold re-verification) — none block close-out.

summary_for_decider: VERIFY-FOCUS PASS. EXP-264 mechanical rework is integrated cleanly: EP2 anchor re-application worked (.ds-verdict-bars .ds-vb-caption literal lands once and CSS rule body inserts after it); all 9 ds- prefix classes are present in both source and built HTML with zero unprefixed leaks; three ink-2 swaps are in place and pass independent WCAG AA verification on dark-mode 7.48:1; Q2 ASCII apostrophe and caption text fixes present; all 5 link targets resolve to existing ids. Two minor follow-ups for tracker (not blocking close-out): (a) audit-only numerical correction in EXP-264 wcag_audit light-mode value 7.69 -> 11.65 (conclusion unchanged, just better than claimed); (b) re-verify above-fold visibility after queue_id 280 (EXP-271) settles, since EXP-271 reorders the surrounding visual flow. Issues 1559/1585/1586/1587/1588 confirmed closed. Recommend pop and close-out.
